title: phlox glaberrima var. suffruticosa
intro: Phlox glaberrima var. suffruticosa, also known as the shrubby phlox, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Phlox glaberrima var. suffruticosa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
